description: Split relational write executor reference-failure handling so only resolver-produced missing document-reference failures can be deferred on the no-profile path.
acceptance-criteria: DefaultRelationalWriteExecutor no longer uses ResolvedReferenceSet.HasFailures to make precedence decisions after reference resolution.
Descriptor-reference failures return the existing reference-failure result immediately.
Non-missing document-reference failures, including incompatible target type, return the existing reference-failure result immediately.
Profile writes with missing document-reference failures stay on the current immediate reference-failure path.
No-profile writes remember resolver-produced DocumentReferenceFailureReason.Missing failures without returning before merge, identity stability, or proposed authorization.
Mixed descriptor and document-reference failures return immediately through the existing reference-failure result shape.
The existing stored relationship/namespace authorization boundary before reference resolution remains in its current position.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/DefaultRelationalWriteExecutor.cs for explicit helper checks over InvalidDescriptorReferences and InvalidDocumentReferences.
Confirm stored relationship/namespace authorization and any If-Match checks that must run before proposed authorization still happen before request-reference resolution.
Confirm no executor precedence branch depends on ResolvedReferenceSet.HasFailures.

description: Add a narrow opt-in that allows the no-profile merge preflight to flatten requests with remembered missing document references for precedence evaluation only.
acceptance-criteria: FlatteningInput has an AllowMissingDocumentReferencesForPrecedence boolean that defaults to false.
RelationalWriteMergeOrchestrator.Resolve accepts an optional allow-missing flag that defaults to false for existing callers.
DefaultRelationalWriteExecutor passes the opt-in only when no-profile missing document-reference failures are being deferred.
The profile merge path never enables the opt-in.
Existing no-profile behavior is unchanged when no missing document-reference failures are deferred.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/RelationalWriteContracts.cs and RelationalWriteMergeOrchestrator.cs for defaulted opt-in plumbing.
Review DefaultRelationalWriteExecutor.cs to confirm the opt-in is tied only to the deferred missing-reference decision.

description: Teach FlatteningResolvedReferenceLookupSet to identify only the exact missing document-reference occurrences produced by the resolver.
acceptance-criteria: When the opt-in is false, FlatteningResolvedReferenceLookupSet does not mark any missing document references as tolerated.
When the opt-in is true, tolerated occurrences are derived from resolvedReferences.InvalidDocumentReferences where Reason is Missing.
Missing failure paths are matched to compiled DocumentReferenceBinding entries by wildcard path and ordinal path.
An invalid missing failure path that cannot be matched to a compiled document-reference binding throws an internal invariant exception.
The lookup exposes a narrow method that answers whether a binding index and ordinal path correspond to a deferred missing document-reference occurrence.
Successful document-reference and descriptor-reference lookup behavior remains unchanged for all non-deferred occurrences.
Callers cannot supply arbitrary tolerated paths or fabricate successful resolved references.
steps-to-verify: Confirm tests cover root and nested ordinal paths, mixed successful and deferred references, and an unmatched failure-path invariant.

description: Update RelationalWriteFlattener so exact deferred missing document-reference occurrences materialize null FK and reference-derived values during no-profile precedence preflight, while all other failures remain fail-fast.
acceptance-criteria: ResolveDocumentReferenceValue returns FlattenedWriteValue.Literal(null) only when the reference object is present, no document id resolved, and the exact binding/ordinal occurrence is marked as deferred missing.

ResolveReferenceDerivedValue returns FlattenedWriteValue.Literal(null) under the same exact-occurrence condition before calling the literal resolver that would otherwise throw for a missing backing reference lookup.
Absent reference objects still materialize null as before and do not require the deferred-missing marker.
Normal flattener mode still throws for present document references that lack a resolved document id.
A present missing reference at a different binding or ordinal path still throws even when another occurrence is deferred.
Descriptor reference flattening behavior is unchanged.
The implementation does not catch or remap unrelated RelationalWriteRequestValidationException or InvalidOperationException failures.
steps-to-verify: Confirm focused tests prove exact deferred occurrences produce nulls and non-deferred missing lookups at other bindings or ordinal paths still fail fast.

description: Return remembered missing document-reference failures only after higher-precedence identity, proposed authorization, and existing deferred If-Match checks have run, and before guarded no-op or persistence.
acceptance-criteria: RelationalWriteIdentityStability.TryBuildFailureResult still runs after merge and before proposed authorization.
Proposed namespace authorization still runs before proposed relationship authorization.
Deferred If-Match precondition evaluation remains in its current location before the deferred missing-reference result.
When no higher-precedence result occurs, remembered missing document-reference failures return through RelationalWriteExecutorResults.BuildReferenceFailureResult.
The deferred reference result rolls back the write session.
Guarded no-op success, PersistAsync, committed representation read, and commit are not reached while deferred missing document-reference failures are remembered.
steps-to-verify: Review DefaultRelationalWriteExecutor.cs to confirm the deferred reference return sits after proposed authorization and deferred If-Match handling but before guarded no-op and PersistAsync.
Confirm focused tests assert PersistAsync is not called and guarded no-op success is not returned when deferred missing references remain.

description: Add the minimal hook that forces create-new POST proposed relationship authorization to run standalone when deferred missing document references will prevent inline insert authorization from executing.
acceptance-criteria: ProposedRelationshipAuthorizationOrchestrator.ResolveAsync accepts an optional forceStandaloneAuthorization parameter that defaults to false.
DefaultRelationalWriteExecutor passes forceStandaloneAuthorization only when missing document-reference failures are deferred.
AuthorizeAsync ignores the create-new POST inline Auth1 shortcut only when forceStandaloneAuthorization is true.
Create-new POST without deferred missing document-reference failures keeps the existing inline Auth1 behavior.
NoClaims and invalid authorization plan handling keep their existing result behavior.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/ProposedRelationshipAuthorizationOrchestrator.cs and DefaultRelationalWriteExecutor.cs for the optional standalone authorization hook.
Confirm tests cover create-new POST with deferred missing references and a successful create-new POST without deferred missing references.

description: Add backend unit coverage for the ODS-shaped write failure precedence matrix without duplicating Core HTTP status mapping tests.
acceptance-criteria: A descriptor-reference failure remains immediate and is not deferred behind a missing document-reference failure.
An incompatible target type document-reference failure remains immediate.
A profile write with a missing document-reference failure remains immediate.
Immutable identity or key-change failure beats a missing document-reference failure.
Proposed namespace authorization failure beats a missing document-reference failure.
Proposed relationship authorization failure beats a missing document-reference failure.
Create-new POST proposed relationship authorization runs standalone before the deferred reference result.
Existing deferred If-Match or precondition failure beats a missing document-reference failure when that precondition branch is applicable.
A missing document-reference failure returns a backend reference-failure result when no higher-precedence failure occurs.
Executor tests assert backend result types and call ordering, not duplicate Core ProblemDetails/status formatting.
steps-to-verify: Review existing Core WriteResultReferenceFailureContractTests only to confirm status mapping remains separately covered.

description: Preserve relational database FK/reference exception mapping as the final race-condition fallback after preflight validation and authorization pass.
acceptance-criteria: RelationalWriteDatabaseFailureResultMapper behavior for FK violations is not removed or weakened.
Resolved references that disappear before persistence can still map to an unresolved-reference write result through the existing DbException catch path.
Deferred missing-reference handling does not hide unrelated flattener, planner, mapping, or database failures.
No new fallback fabricates successful reference resolution or suppresses unexpected write exceptions.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/RelationalWriteDatabaseFailureResultMapper.cs and the DbException catch in DefaultRelationalWriteExecutor.cs.
Confirm no new catch block maps unrelated InvalidOperationException or validation failures to unresolved-reference results.

description: Flip only the E2E scenarios named in the 409 fix plan whose relational behavior now matches ODS precedence, and validate them through the relational build script path.
acceptance-criteria: UpdateReferenceValidation.feature scenario 03 is enabled for relational backend with descriptor-validation precedence expectations, and scenario 04 expects 400 Key Change Not Supported instead of an early 409 after this fix.
RelationshipsWithEdOrgsAndContacts.feature scenario 20 is enabled for relational backend only when it returns 403 Authorization Denied instead of an early 409.

RelationshipsWithEdOrgsAndPeople.feature scenarios 32, 39, and 40 are enabled for relational backend only when their relational behavior matches the ODS-shaped authorization/precedence result described in 409-fix-plan.md; scenarios 39 and 40 expect 403 Authorization Denied, and scenario 32 records its exact expected status and body explicitly in the feature.

RelationshipsWithEdOrgsAndStaffs.feature scenarios 03 and 15 are enabled for relational backend with key-change-before-update-authorization expectations because the current relational behavior already matches ODS precedence.
Each newly enabled scenario has @relational-backend and the appropriate @relational-ci-shard-* tag before it is counted as covered by the relational E2E run.
No unrelated E2E scenarios receive expectation changes, relational-backend tags, or shard tags as part of this fix.

Shard tags for newly enabled scenarios follow the neighboring feature-file convention for the same test lane: authorization scenarios use shard 3 and UpdateReferenceValidation scenarios use shard 4 in the current repo, unless the feature file convention changes during implementation.
The relational E2E run uses ./build-dms.ps1 E2ETest with ./.env.e2e.relational rather than direct setup-local-dms.ps1 plus dotnet test.
Any environment-only E2E failure is documented with Docker/runtime evidence rather than treated as a product failure.
steps-to-verify: From the repository root, run ./build-dms.ps1 E2ETest -Configuration Release -SkipDockerBuild -IdentityProvider self-contained -EnvironmentFile './.env.e2e.relational' -TestFilter 'Category=@relational-backend&Category=@relational-ci-shard-3'

From the repository root, run ./build-dms.ps1 E2ETest -Configuration Release -SkipDockerBuild -IdentityProvider self-contained -EnvironmentFile './.env.e2e.relational' -TestFilter 'Category=@relational-backend&Category=@relational-ci-shard-4' for the newly enabled UpdateReferenceValidation scenarios, or use the updated shard tag if the feature file convention changes during implementation.
If the E2E run fails before issuing API requests, inspect dms-local Docker logs and the stack environment before classifying the result.

description: Format and run the focused validation suite for the relational write precedence fix without broad unrelated refactoring.
acceptance-criteria: All modified C# files under src/dms are formatted with csharpier.
Focused backend unit tests for executor precedence, flattener deferred missing-reference behavior, and database fallback preservation pass.
Core reference-failure status mapping tests still pass without new handler behavior.
ProblemDetails wording and Core response/status mapping remain unchanged.
The relational E2E shards containing the named authorization and reference scenarios pass, or any environment-only failure is documented with supporting evidence.
tasks.json remains valid JSON and no production or test source changes outside this story scope are included.
steps-to-verify: Run ./build-dms.ps1 E2ETest -Configuration Release -SkipDockerBuild -IdentityProvider self-contained -EnvironmentFile './.env.e2e.relational' -TestFilter 'Category=@relational-backend&Category=@relational-ci-shard-4' for the newly enabled UpdateReferenceValidation scenarios, or use the updated shard tag if the feature file convention changes during implementation.
